metadata.description: Aboard the Faraday. The ship's common room, cramped and smelling of recycled air and engine grease. Star charts pinned to every flat surface.

content_hints.description: The Faraday's common room. Bolted-down benches ring a central holo-table that flickers with the blue ghost of a star chart. Cable bundles run along the ceiling like mechanical veins. Someone has taped a faded pin-up from Rin's Cantina over a hull patch that's been welded twice.
content_hints.read_aloud: Citani's projection stabilizes on the holo-table. Their face is its usual mask of professional neutrality, but their eyes are sharper than usual. 'I have a job. Pays well. Short window. The Ashen Knives have product to move and Hegemonic attention to avoid.'

npcs.name: Citani
npcs.dialogue: The Ashen Knives have a large shipment of Memish psychedelics. Hegemonic patrols are heavy on the route to Warren. The Knives are friendly -- for now -- but that's conditional on you not screwing this up. The plan is deception: forged manifests. Captain, this is your show.
npcs.notes: Citani (they/them) is the crew's primary info broker. Operates through encrypted holo-channels only. Has never been seen in person by anyone aboard. Their intel is always good, but their price goes up every time. The Ashen Knives are +1 Friendly with the crew.

event_triggers: Citani transmits encrypted coordinates and a manifest template. 'Forge it well. Hegemonic customs at the Warren gate are running double shifts since the Yaru incident. You get flagged, you're on your own.'

content_hints.description: The engine bay. Hayley Griffen's domain. Tools hang from magnetic strips in precise rows. The Faraday's main drive hums behind its housing like a caged animal. Griffen has left a diagnostic running -- green lights cascade across a wall panel in rhythmic pulses.

npcs.name: Hayley Griffen
npcs.dialogue: The drive's tuned. Transponder's clean. I can rig a secondary ident beacon if you want a fallback -- takes an hour and a bottle of that Ishtari rum Aleera's been hiding.
npcs.notes: Hayley Griffen, the Faraday's mechanic. Notable quality: Fixed. She cannot leave a broken thing alone. Currently nursing a grudge against the portside attitude thruster, which she insists has a personality.

content_hints.description: Crew quarters. Four bunks, three occupied by personal effects that define their owners -- Aleera's flight manual dog-eared to the chapter on evasive maneuvers, Rogan's meditation beads hung from a bunk strut, Ray's collection of forged credentials spread across his mattress like a hand of cards.
